docs.title: Church Governance Documents
docs.text:
Welcome to the official repository for the governance documents of the church.

This site serves as a centralized reference for the church’s constitution, bylaws, policies, and job descriptions. These documents exist to support faithful ministry, ensure good order, and provide clarity for leadership, staff, and members.

docs.title: Purpose of This Site
docs.text:
The documents published here are intended to:

  • Promote transparency and accountability in church governance
  • Provide clear guidance for leadership roles and responsibilities
  • Preserve governing documents in a stable, accessible format
  • Serve as a living reference that can be updated as the church grows

Unless otherwise noted, these documents represent the current, adopted policies and governing standards of the church.

docs.title: Document Sections
docs.text:
Use the navigation menu to access the following sections:

  • Constitution The foundational principles, doctrinal statements, and organizational structure of the church.

  • Bylaws Operating rules and procedures governing church life, leadership, and decision-making.

  • Policies Approved policies addressing ministry operations, safety, finance, facilities, and conduct.

  • Job Descriptions Defined roles, responsibilities, and expectations for staff and key volunteer positions.

docs.title: Versioning and Updates
docs.text:
These documents may be revised from time to time by the appropriate governing body. When revisions occur, updates will be reflected here to ensure this site remains the most current reference.

For questions regarding interpretation or application of these documents, please consult church leadership.

“Let all things be done decently and in order.” — 1 Corinthians 14:40

docs.title: Bylaws
docs.text:
The Bylaws provide the operational rules by which the church functions on a day-to-day basis.

They establish procedures for decision-making, leadership responsibilities, meetings, and administration in accordance with the Constitution.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The Bylaws typically address:

  • Leadership roles and responsibilities
  • Church officers and committees
  • Business meetings and voting procedures
  • Financial oversight and administration
  • Amendment procedures

docs.title: Relationship to the Constitution
docs.text: The Bylaws operate under the authority of the Constitution and may not conflict with it. In the event of a conflict, the Constitution shall prevail.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Church Constitution
  • Church Policies

docs.title: By-Laws
docs.text:
Version 1.4

BY-LAWS OF FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF CORSICANA, TEXAS

docs.title: ARTICLE ONE. NAME AND PURPOSE
docs.text: The name and purpose of this organization shall be as set forth in the constitution adopted on May 5, 2004.

docs.title: Section A. Admission
docs.text:
Admission to membership in this body shall be upon majority vote of the members present and voting in any regularly scheduled meeting based upon the satisfaction of one of the following criteria:

  1. Upon profession of faith in the Lord Jesus Christ as Saviour and baptism by immersion in the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit;

  2. Upon transfer of letter from some other Baptist church of like faith and order;

  3. Upon restoration to membership if, for various reasons, the name of an individual has been dropped from our roll;

  4. By personal statement of a Baptist having been baptized by immersion into the fellowship of a Baptist church and whose church connection or church letter has been lost;

  5. By personal statement of an individual that he or she is already a Christian and was immersed by a church of like faith and order as set forth in 1 above.

docs.title: Section B. Watchcare
docs.text: Individuals may be received under the watchcare of the church for the purpose of worshiping regularly with our fellowship with no privilege of voting (designed primarily for non-Baptists).

docs.title: Section C. Termination
docs.text:
Names of members may be removed from the roll in any of the following ways:

  1. Upon due request from another Baptist church of like faith and order, the church may grant letters at its discretion;

  2. The name of a member will be removed upon the member's death;

  3. The name of a member who united with a church of another denomination may be removed from the roll by erasure when this church is notified by the member or by the church with which the member has united;

  4. A member may be dismissed from membership as a method of church discipline provided a three-fourths (3/4) majority of the members present and voting at a regularly scheduled business meeting of the church favor withdrawal of fellowship.

docs.title: Section A.
docs.text: The church shall hold regular meetings for the purpose of worship, prayer, teaching, training and fellowship on Sunday mornings, Sunday evenings, and Wednesday evenings. Other meetings of the church as a whole shall be set according to the needs of the church.

docs.title: Section B.
docs.text: A church conference shall be held on the third ~~Wednesday~~ Sunday¹² evening following the first Sunday in each month (unless otherwise scheduled) at which time written reports will be received from the church clerk, the Finance Committee, and from such other standing committees or ministry teams as the church may require from time to time. When a report is to be made by a special committee appointed for a specific or limited purpose, prior notice of the intent to present the report will be given the membership by means of announcement in the church’s regular publication. Any other business of the church may be considered at these monthly conferences without advance notice except as otherwise specified in the constitution or elsewhere in the by-laws.

docs.title: Section C.
docs.text: A special church conference may be called by the Pastor, the Chairman of Deacons or by majority vote of the members present and voting at a regularly scheduled worship meeting provided that written notice shall be given in the church's regular publication at least one week in advance of the special session, stating the purpose of said meeting. A special church conference may also be called during any regular Sunday or Wednesday meeting by the joint consent of the Pastor and the Chairman of Deacons with the unanimous consent of the members present and voting.

docs.title: Section D.
docs.text: All church conferences shall be conducted in accordance with standard parliamentary procedure as described in Robert’s Rules of Order.

docs.title: Section E.
docs.text: In the event of an emergency (fire, storm, natural disaster, etc.), the Pastor and/or Chairman of Deacons is authorized to make decisions about the use of the church's property and/or resources to meet the particular emergency.

docs.title: SECTION 1. ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICERS
docs.text: The Pastor, the Church Moderator, the Church Clerk, and the Church Treasurer shall constitute the Administrative Officers of the church. The Pastor shall hold the position of Church Moderator if he so desires. Any person elected to one of these official positions may, in addition, serve as a corporate officer.

docs.title: SECTION 2. CORPORATE OFFICERS
docs.text:
A. Corporate Officers. ~~Should the church become a corporate body,~~ the corporate officers of the church shall include a president, two vice-presidents, a secretary and a treasurer. The corporate treasurer shall also be the church treasurer. The corporate officers shall be nominated by the Committee on Committees and elected by the church. Terms of service shall be three years with one or two officers rotating off each year as with standing committees.

~~B. Trustees. The church shall elect three trustees to serve as representatives for the church in legal matters. They shall remain in office until they resign or the church names a replacement.³~~

C. The church incorporated primarily to protect the membership at large from legal liability. The corporate officers shall therefore be authorized to review policies and procedures and to assess any potential risk that might arise from the day-to-day operations of the church. The results of such an audit shall be presented to the church with appropriate recommendations for action to mitigate the potential risk.

D. The corporate officers shall assume the role formerly held by the trustees of the church. That is, they shall represent the church in legal matters.

E. The corporate officers shall initiate recommendations to the church regarding the usage or placement of funds other than those that are budgeted or designated.

F. A Church Moderator (if the Pastor chooses not to serve in this capacity) shall be elected annually by the church to serve for a period of one year, the term to begin on January 1 following election. In the event the Moderator is absent from any regular or special church conference, the Chairman of Deacons shall preside, or the members present and voting shall elect a temporary moderator to preside over that particular conference. The Moderator shall preside over the regular and special church conferences described in these by-laws and the constitution.

G. The terms of service of the administrative and corporate officers shall be one year or until successors are duly elected. The Pastor is excluded from this provision and shall be elected for an unspecified term of office.

docs.title: SECTION 1. ELECTION
docs.text: The church shall elect from its membership deacons in a number the church deems necessary to fill its needs. Deacons will be elected by the church in regular conference upon recommendation of the Deacon Body. The Deacon Body shall elect its own officers including, but not limited to, a Chairman, a Vice-Chairman and Secretary.

docs.title: SECTION 2. DUTIES
docs.text:
In accordance with the meaning of the word deacon and the example of the New Testament churches, deacons are to be leader-servants of the church. The following principles of service (based on Acts 6) define the purpose for deacons:

A. To assist the ministerial staff with secondary responsibilities in order that they may spend more time in prayer and the ministry of the Word. (For example, the visitation ministry, aid church committees in administration matters, ministry to the sick and needy, etc.) All recommendations from church committees and ministry teams will be presented to the Deacon Council for approval and then forwarded to the church for final determination.

B. To give attention to the needs of the membership, especially to widows.

C. To assist in resolving problems which arise within the fellowship and work toward unity.

D. To be supportive of the God-called leadership.

E. To be examples within the fellowship of wisdom, spirituality, faithfulness and good character.

docs.title: ARTICLE SIX. STANDING COMMITTEES
docs.text: All standing committees shall be elected by the church in regular conference. Terms of service shall be three years, and approximately one-third (1/3) of the members of each committee shall be elected annually in order that continuity be maintained in the work of the committees. A member who has served a three-year term on a committee is not eligible for election to any committee until at least one year has elapsed. A member of the church staff shall serve as advisor for each standing committee.

docs.title: SECTION 1. COMMITTEE ON COMMITTEES
docs.text: The Committee on Committees shall be elected by the church in regular conference. One-third of the committee shall be elected each year from nominations made by the members prior to the conference. This committee shall bring recommendations to the church for members of all standing committees except for the Committee on Committees.

docs.title: SECTION 2. FINANCE COMMITTEE
docs.text:
The Finance Committee shall perform the following duties:

a. Budget expenditures: review financial expenditures each month, evaluate requests for special offerings by church organizations, and keep the church membership informed regarding receipts and expenditures.

b. Monthly reports: they shall report to the church at regular monthly church conferences on receipts and expenditures through the budget and any other authorized funds.

c. Budget preparation: along with the Church Council, they shall study expenditures and needs, estimate giving potential of the church, prepare an achievable budget for the coming year and present it to the church for its consideration.

docs.title: SECTION 3. PROPERTIES COMMITTEE
docs.text: This committee shall be entrusted with the responsibility of physical property, upkeep of current and future buildings, operation of oversight of physical plant needs. This shall include buildings, furnishings and permanently installed equipment.

docs.title: SECTION 4. PERSONNEL COMMITTEE
docs.text: The Personnel Committee shall assist the Pastor in matters related to personnel administration and management including such areas as employment, salaries, benefits, personnel and services, and preparation of job descriptions.

docs.title: SECTION 5. OTHER COMMITTEES
docs.text: Other standing committees may be elected to fill specific needs and shall operate under the same general provisions as described in this article for the committees specifically named. Such committees may be added, continued or discontinued as the church sees fit.

docs.title: ARTICLE SEVEN. PASTOR SUPPLY / SEARCH COMMITTEES
docs.text: In the event that the office of Pastor shall become vacant, two special committees shall be elected: a Pulpit Supply Committee, and a Pastor Search Committee.

docs.title: SECTION 1. PULPIT SUPPLY COMMITTEE
docs.text:
A. Selection The Pulpit Supply Committee shall be nominated by the Committee on Committees and elected by the church. The committee shall not consist of more than seven persons.

B. Duties 1. The Pulpit Supply Committee shall be responsible for securing an appropriate speaker for each worship service until more permanent arrangements are made. 2. The Pulpit Supply Committee shall, if appropriate and desired by the church, recommend to the church for its consideration a person to serve as Interim Pastor until such time as a pastor is called.

docs.title: SECTION 2. PASTOR SEARCH COMMITTEE
docs.text:
A. Selection 1. The Pastor Search Committee shall consist of a membership of seven active members (four men and three women) and one alternate who will be privileged to attend meetings and who will be available to fill any vacancy which may occur on the Pastor Search Committee. 2. The committee members will be selected under the supervision of the Personnel Committee as follows: 3. Every church member shall have the opportunity to submit a single, written and signed list of nominations of not more than five persons for election to the Pastor Search Committee. 4. The Personnel Committee will, from these nominations, prepare a ballot of thirty nominees, observing the following criteria: a. Give primary consideration to the number of nominations each person received. b. No two members of the same family or related family shall be elected to the Pastor Search Committee. The man or woman receiving the lesser number of votes shall be dropped and the next highest man or woman shall be elected. c. No church staff member or member of the household of any church staff member shall be placed on the ballot. d. All nominees must be members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana, twenty-one years of age or older and must, in advance, consent to serve if elected. 5. The ballot of thirty nominees will be published in the regular church publication and the election will be held at the Sunday morning service on the Sunday after the publication date. Each church member will have the opportunity to cast a written ballot for seven nominees (four men and three women). All ballots shall be signed. The seven nominees receiving the highest number of votes shall constitute the Pastor Search Committee. If an alternate is needed, the one receiving the next highest number of votes will serve as alternate (either male or female).

B. Duties 1. The Pastor Search Committee, under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, shall have the duty of seeking out and, after prayerful consideration, recommending to the church a person to be called to serve as Pastor. In the process of fulfilling this responsibility, the Pastor Search Committee will work closely with the Finance Committee, the Personnel Committee, and such other committees as may be necessary. 2. After its election, the Pastor Search Committee will meet, select its own chairman and organize itself to fulfill its duties. The Pastor Search Committee shall make periodic reports to the church in conference on its progress. 3. The work of the Pastor Search Committee is complete when a Pastor has been called, has accepted and has arrived on the field. The Pastor Search Committee shall then be automatically dissolved.

docs.title: ARTICLE EIGHT. SELECTION OF OTHER STAFF
docs.text:
A. Selection of other staff positions (Education, Music, Youth, et. al.) shall be done through the service of the Personnel Committee plus additional members chosen from the church-at-large to form a Search Committee not to exceed a total of nine (9) persons. Nominations for three additional members may be made from the floor of the church in conference. People nominated must grant their permission to serve on a search committee.⁴ The three receiving the highest number of votes shall be included in the search committee.

B. The committee shall select its own chairman and organize to fulfill its duties. They shall make periodic reports to the church in conference on its progress. The committee will work closely with the Finance Committee and other leadership in the area of work (S.S., Music, etc.) as may be necessary.

C. This committee shall function until such time as they bring a recommendation to the church and the selection of a new staff member is made after which they will be dissolved as a committee.

docs.title: ARTICLE NINE. SELECTION OF MESSENGERS
docs.text:
Messengers to the annual meetings of the Corsicana Baptist Association, Baptist General Convention of Texas and the Southern Baptist Convention shall be elected by the church in accordance with the following procedure:

A. Persons who are willing to be considered as messengers to any of these annual meetings shall inform the church office of their willingness to serve at least thirty days prior to the scheduled meeting for which they are being considered for election as messengers.

B. The requests shall be referred to the Committee on Committees. The committee shall, in the last regularly scheduled business meeting before the date that messengers must be appointed in order to be eligible to attend the applicable convention, present to the church its recommendations of persons to be elected as messengers and alternates.

C. If full allotment of messengers has not been reached, additional nominations may be made from the floor if desired, and the church in conference will elect the appropriate number of messengers and alternates.

docs.title: ARTICLE TEN. AMENDMENTS
docs.text:
These by-laws may be amended by the following procedure:

A. Any proposed amendment shall be offered in writing at the regular church conference.

B. The proposed amendment, together with the designated time for its consideration, shall be published in two consecutive issues of the church's official weekly publication thirty days prior to the meeting in which a vote is to be taken.

C. The proposed amendment shall be approved by three-fourths (3/4) of the members present and voting at each of two consecutive regular business meetings of the church after proper offering and notification described above.

Amendment Notes / Footnotes

¹ Amendment Approved. First Vote June 14, 2020. Second Vote July 12, 2020. Version 1.4 ² Amendment Approved. First Vote November 11, 2011. Second Vote December 7, 2011. Version 1.3 ³ Church Incorporated March 8, 2006. Version 1.2 ⁴ Added. Church Conference December 8, 2004. Version 1.1

First Baptist Corsicana

docs.title: By-Laws of First Baptist Church of Corsicana, Texas<sup>1</sup>
Article One. Name and Purpose
docs.text: The name and purpose of this organization shall be as set forth in the constitution adopted on May 5, 2004.

docs.title: Section A. Admission
docs.text:
Admission to membership in this body shall be upon majority vote of the members present and voting in any regularly scheduled meeting based upon the satisfaction of one of the following criteria:

  1. Upon profession of faith in the Lord Jesus Christ as Savior, and baptism by immersion in the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit;
  2. Upon transfer of letter from some other Baptist church of like faith and order;
  3. Upon restoration to membership, if, for various reasons, the name of an individual has been dropped from our roll;
  4. By personal statement of a Baptist, having been baptized by immersion into the fellowship of a Baptist church and whose church connection or church letter has been lost;
  5. By personal statement of an individual that he or she is already a Christian and was immersed by a church of like faith and order as set forth in #1 above.

docs.title: Section B. Watchcare
docs.text: Individuals may be received under the watchcare of the church for the purpose of worshipping regularly with our fellowship with no privilege of voting (designed primarily for non-Baptists).

docs.title: Section C. Termination
docs.text:
Names of members may be removed from the roll in any of the following ways:

  1. Upon request from another Baptist church of like faith and order, the church may grant letters at its discretion;
  2. The name of a member will be removed upon the member's death;
  3. The name of a member who united with a church of another denomination may be removed from the roll by erasure when this church is notified by the member or by the church with which the member has united;
  4. A member may be dismissed from membership as a method of church discipline provided a three-fourths (3/4) majority of the members present and voting at a regularly scheduled business meeting of the church body in favor of withdrawal of fellowship.

docs.title: Section A
docs.text: The church shall hold regular meetings for the purpose of worship, prayer, teaching, training and fellowship on Sunday mornings, Sunday evenings, and Wednesday evenings. Other meetings of the church as a whole shall be set according to the needs of the church.

docs.title: Section B
docs.text: A church conference will be held on the third2 Sunday3 evening in each month (unless otherwise scheduled), at which time written reports will be received from the church clerk, The Finance Committee, and from the other standing committees or ministry teams as the church may require from time to time. When a report is to be made by a special committee appointed for a specific or limited purpose, prior notice of the intent to present the report will be given the membership by means of announcement in the church's regular publication. Any other business of the church may be considered at these monthly conferences without advance notice except as otherwise specified in the constitution or elsewhere in the by-laws.

docs.title: Section C
docs.text: A special church conference may be called by the Senior Pastor, the Chairman of the Deacons, or by majority vote of the members present and voting at a regularly scheduled worship meeting provided that written notice shall be given in the church's regular publication at least one week in advance of the special session stating the purpose of said meeting. A special church conference may also be called during any regular Sunday or Wednesday meeting by joint consent of the Pastor and the Chairman of the Deacons with the unanimous consent of the members present and voting.

docs.title: Section D
docs.text: All church conferences and meetings shall be conducted in accordance with standard parliamentary procedures as described in the latest edition of Robert's Rules of Order.

docs.title: Section E
docs.text: In the event of an emergency (fire, storm, natural disaster, etc.), the Pastor, and/or the Chairman of the Deacons is authorized to make decisions about the use of the church's property and/or resources to meet the particular emergency.

docs.title: Section A. Administrative Officers
docs.text:
The Senior Pastor, the Church Moderator, the Church Clerk, and the Church Treasurer shall constitute the Administrative Officers of the church. The term of service for the Church Clerk and the Church Treasurer will be for three (3) years.

  1. The Pastor shall hold the position of Church Moderator if he so desires. If the Pastor chooses not to be the Church Moderator, a Church Moderator will be elected annually by the church to serve for a period of one year, the term to begin on January 1st, following election. In the event the Moderator is absent from any regular or special church conference, the Chairman of the Deacons shall preside, or the members present and voting shall elect a temporary moderator to preside over that particular conference. The Moderator shall preside over the regular and special church conferences described in these by-laws and the constitution.

docs.title: Section B. Corporate Officers
docs.text:
1. The Corporate Officers of the church will consist of five members, which shall include a president, two vice presidents, a secretary, and a treasurer. The Corporate treasurer shall also be the church treasurer. The Corporate secretary will serve as the church clerk. The corporate officers shall be nominated by the Committee on Committees and elected by the church. Terms of service shall be three years with one or two officers rotating off each year as with standing committees. Those nominated and desiring to serve as corporate officers shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of four years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership.
  2. The corporate officers shall be authorized to review policies and procedures, and to assess any potential risk that might arise from the day-to-day operations of the church with appropriate recommendations for action to mitigate the potential risk.
  3. The corporate officers will sign legal documents when necessary, and will consult with outside legal counsel when necessary to protect themselves and the church.
  4. The Corporate Officers shall initiate recommendations to the church, or receive recommendations from committees, regarding the usage or placement of funds other than those that are budgeted or designated.

docs.title: Section A. Election
docs.text: The church shall elect from its membership, deacons in a number the church deems necessary to fill its needs. Deacons will be elected by the church in regular conference upon recommendation of the Deacon body. Those nominated and desiring to serve as deacons shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of 2 years (if previously ordained, or 3 years for those yet to be ordained). The date of membership entered into the membership database will be used to establish length of church membership. The Deacon body shall elect its own officers including, but not limited to, a Chairman, a Vice-Chairman, and a Secretary

docs.title: Section B. Duties
docs.text:
In accordance with the scriptural meaning of the word "deacon" and the example of the New Testament churches, deacons are to be leader-servants of the church. The following principles of service, based on Acts 6, define the purpose for deacons:

  1. To give attention to the needs of the membership, especially to widows.
  2. To assist the ministerial staff with secondary responsibilities in order that they may spend more time in prayer and in the ministry of the Word. (For example the visitation ministry, aid church committees in administration matters, ministry to the sick and needy, etc.) Recommendations from church committees and ministry teams will be presented to the Deacon body for consideration and then forwarded to the church for final acceptance or rejection.
  3. To assist in resolving problems which arise within the fellowship and work toward unity.
  4. To be supportive of the God-called leadership.
  5. To be examples within the fellowship of wisdom, spirituality, faithfulness and good character, and other examples in 1 Timothy 3:8-10.

docs.title: Section C. Accountability
docs.text:
REFER TO 1 Timothy 3:8-10

With regards to attendance at deacon meetings, the following steps are designed to encourage the participation of the deacon body in monthly meetings and other deacon responsibilities.

  1. If unable to attend the monthly meetings, a deacon is responsible to contact an officer to inform him of his absence. That absence will be designated as an excused absence.
  2. If a deacon does not attend a monthly meeting and has not been given an excused absence, his absence will be considered an unexcused absence.
  3. In the event the deacon has three consecutive unexcused absences, the deacon will automatically be removed from the deacon roster.
  4. The deacon that has been removed from the deacon roster, remains an ordained deacon of the church.
  5. To restore a deacon's status on the deacon roster, he must request so in writing, and request an interview with the fellowship committee. There must be approval of reinstatement by a majority vote of the deacon body.

docs.title: Article Six. Standing Committees
docs.text: All standing committees shall be elected by the church in regular conference. Terms of service shall be three years, and approximately one-third (1/3) of the members of each committee shall be elected annually, in order that continuity be maintained in the work of the committees. A member who has served a three-year term on a committee is not eligible for election to any committee until at least one year has lapsed. A member of the Ministerial Staff will serve as advisor and ex-officio member for each standing committee as assigned by the Senior Pastor.

docs.title: Section A. Committee on Committees
docs.text: The Committee on Committees (comprised of 9 members) shall be elected by the church in regular conference from nominations made by church members, prior to the conference in September. One-third of the committee shall be elected each year from nominations made by members. A ministerial staff member will serve as an advisor/ex-officio member. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Committee on Committees shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of four years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership.This committee will bring recommendations to the church for members of all standing committees except for the Committee on Committees. Service will begin in October of each year.

docs.title: Section B. Finance Committee
docs.text:
Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Finance Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of two years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership. The Finance Committee will be comprised of nine (9) voting members. The financial secretary, the church treasurer, and a member of the ministerial staff shall serve as ex-officio members of the committee. The Finance Committee shall perform the following duties:

  1. Budget Expenditures: review financial expenditures each month, evaluate requests for special offerings by church organizations, and keep the church membership informed regarding receipts and expenditures.
  2. Monthly Reports: they shall report to the church at regular monthly church conferences on receipts and expenditures through the budget and any other authorized funds.
  3. Budget preparation: along with the *Budget Committee, they shall study expenditures and needs, estimate giving potential of the church, prepare an achievable budget for the coming year and present it to the church for its consideration.
  4. Shall review expenditures not included in the budget, relative to the availability of funds and potential impact on overall church finances, and make appropriate recommendations to the membership regarding temporary investment of excess funds.

docs.title: Section C. Properties Committee
docs.text:
1. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Properties Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of one (1) year at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership.
  2. Membership of the Properties Committee is composed of six (6) members, and a ministerial staff person who serves as the advisor/ex-officio member.
  3. Properties Committee shall be entrusted with the responsibility of physical property, upkeep of current and future buildings and the operation of oversight of physical plant needs. This shall include buildings, furnishings, and permanently installed equipment and grounds.
  4. Properties Committee shall be responsible for oversight of the maintenance of all church properties, keeping them ready for use.

docs.title: Section D. Personnel Committee
docs.text:
1. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Personnel Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of four (4) years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of membership.
  2. Membership of the Personnel Committee is composed of six (6) members, and a ministerial staff person who serves as the advisor/ex-officio member.
  3. The Personnel Committee shall assist the Senior Pastor in matters related to personnel administration and management, including such areas as employment, salaries, benefits, personnel and services, and preparation of job descriptions.

docs.title: Section E. Missions Committee
docs.text:
1. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Missions Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of one (1) year at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church member database will be used to establish the length of membership.
  2. The Missions Committee, composed of six members, serving three (3) year terms, is charged with managing the monies that are designated for missions causes. The committee will work alongside a member of the ministerial staff to make recommendations to the church for the investment of these monies for mission trips and for mission work in Navarro County, in Texas, and around the world. Terms of service will begin in January.
  3. Each member of the Missions Committee must show personal conviction concerning mission work and a compassion for ministering to the hurting, and reaching the lost.
  4. The Missions Committee will bring recommendations before the church, concerning the use of undesignated monies for the support of missionaries on the field, mission causes around the world, and mission trips from First Baptist Church of Corsicana.
  5. The Missions Committee will work with the Finance Committee to provide financial support for mission work in the Corsicana area, in Texas, in the U.S., and around the world.
  6. The Chair of the Missions Committee will participate in the budget process for the support of mission causes through the annual budget.
  7. The Missions Committee shall bring recommendations concerning the goals for Mission offerings.
  8. The Missions Committee will assist and advise the Senior Pastoral staff concerning mission speakers and emphases that are designed to raise financial support.
  9. The Missions Committee does not have oversight of mission activities within the church. (i.e., Youth, Hispanic, etc.)
  10. The Missions Committee will meet quarterly or as needed during the year.

docs.title: Section F. Transportation Committee
docs.text:
The Transportation Committee consisting of three(3) members, and a ministerial staff member who will be an advisor/ex-officio member, shall be in direct control of all church-owned vehicles. They will formulate policies concerning the use of all church vehicles and update these policies when necessary. All policies shall be approved by the church.

  1. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Transportation Committee shall be active, and participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of one (1) year at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership.
  2. The Transportation Committee will ensure that all church vehicles receive regular and proper maintenance.
  3. The Transportation Committee shall recommend to the church for approval, all purchases and disposition of church vehicles.
  4. The Transportation Committee shall make recommendations to the Finance/Budget Committee, concerning its area of need and responsibility for the year.
  5. The Transportation Committee will meet as needed.

docs.title: Section G. Other Special Committees
docs.text:
Committees may be elected to fill specific needs (other than Pulpit Supply and Pastor Search) and shall operate under the same general provisions as described in these articles for the committees specifically named. Such committees may be added, continued or discontinued as the church sees fit.

  1. Those nominated and desiring to serve on a Special Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of three (3) years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish the length of church membership.

docs.title: Article Seven. Pastor Supply and Search Committees
docs.text: In the event the office of Senior Pastor shall become vacant, two special committees shall be elected: a Pulpit Supply Committee and a Pastor Search Committee.

docs.title: Section A. Pulpit Supply Committee
docs.text:
1. Selection: The Pulpit Supply Committee shall be nominated by the Committee on Committees and elected by the church. Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Pulpit Supply Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana for a minimum of four (4) years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church membership database will be used to establish length of church membership. The committee shall consist of seven (7) members.
  2. Duties: a. The Pulpit Supply Committee shall be responsible for securing an appropriate speaker for each worship service until more permanent arrangements can be made. b. The Pulpit Supply Committee shall, if appropriate, and desired by the church, recommend to the church for its consideration a person to serve as interim Pastor until such time as a Senior Pastor is called.

docs.title: Section B. Senior Pastor Search Committee
docs.text:
1. Selection: a. The Senior Pastor Search Committee shall consist of seven (7) active members and one alternate who will attend meetings and who will be available to fill any vacancy which may occur on the Pastor Search Committee. b. The committee members will be selected under the supervision of the Personnel Committee as follows: c. Every church member shall have the opportunity to submit a single, written and signed list of nominations of not more than five persons for election to the Senior Pastor Search Committee. The Personnel Committee will, from those nominations, prepare a ballot of thirty nominees, observing the following criteria: (i) All nominees must be 21 years of age or older. Nominees must, in advance, give consent to serve if elected. (ii) Those nominated and desiring to serve on the Senior Pastor Search Committee shall be active, participating members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana, for a minimum of four (4) years at the time the election is held. The date of membership entered into the church database of membership will be used to establish length of church membership. (iii) Give primary consideration to the number of nominations each person received. (iv) No two members of the same family or related family shall be elected to the Senior Pastor Search Committee. The related man or woman receiving the lesser number of votes shall be dropped and the next highest man or woman shall be elected. (v) No church staff member or member of the household of a staff member shall be placed on the ballot. d. The ballot of thirty nominees will be published in the regular church publication and the election will be held at the Sunday morning service on the Sunday after the publication date. Each church member will have the opportunity to cast a ballot for the seven nominees. All ballots will be signed. The seven nominees receiving the highest number of votes shall constitute the Senior Pastor Search Committee. The person receiving the next highest number of votes will be the alternate.
  2. Duties: a. The Senior Pastor Search Committee, under the guidance of the Holy Spirit, shall consider and recommend to the church a person to be called to serve as Senior Pastor. The Senior Pastor Search Committee will work closely with the Finance Committee, the Personnel Committee, and other such committees as may be necessary. b. After its election, the Senior Pastor Search Committee will meet, select its own chairman, and organize itself to fulfill its duties. The Senior Pastor Search Committee shall make periodic reports to the church in conference on its progress. c. The work of the Senior Pastor Search Committee will be considered complete when a Senior Pastor has been called, has accepted and arrived on the field. The Senior Pastor Search Committee shall then be dissolved.

docs.title: Article Eight. Selection of Other Ministerial Staff
Section A
docs.text: The selection of other ministerial staff positions (Education, Music, Youth, Hispanic Minister, et. al.) shall be done through the service of the Personnel Committee and three members chosen from the church at large to form a search committee not to exceed a total of nine voting individuals (members). Nominations for the three additional members may be made from the floor of the church in conference. Those nominated must grant their permission to serve on a committee. The three receiving the highest number of votes shall be included on the Other Ministerial Staff Search Committee.

docs.title: Section B
docs.text: The Committee shall elect its own chairman and organize to fulfill its duties. They shall make periodic reports to the church in conference on its progress. The committee will work closely with the Finance Committee and other leadership in the areas of work (S.S., Music, Youth, Hispanic Ministry, etc.) as may be necessary.

docs.title: Section C
docs.text: This committee shall function until such time as they bring a recommendation to the church and the selection of a new staff member is made, after which they will be dissolved as a committee. The scope of the search of this committee will be confined to the specific position being filled.

docs.title: Section A
docs.text:
Messengers to the annual meeting of the Baptist General Convention of Texas shall be elected by the church in accordance with the following procedure:

  1. Persons who are willing to be considered as messengers to any of these annual meetings shall inform the church office of their willingness to serve, at least thirty days prior to the scheduled meeting for which they are being considered as messengers.
  2. The names of volunteers shall be given to the Committee on Committees who shall, in the last regularly scheduled business meeting before the date that messengers must be appointed to be eligible to attend the applicable convention, present to the church its recommendations of persons to be elected as messengers and alternates.
  3. If full allotment of messengers has not been reached, additional nominations may be made from the floor if desired, and the church in conference will elect the appropriate number of messengers and alternates.

docs.title: Section A
docs.text:
These By-Laws may be amended by the following procedure:

  1. Any proposed amendment shall be offered in writing at the regular church conference.
  2. The proposed amendment, together with the designated time for its consideration, shall be published in two consecutive issues of the church's official weekly publication thirty days prior to the meeting in which the vote is to be taken.
  3. The proposed amendment shall be approved by three fourths (3/4) of the members present and voting at each of two consecutive regular business meetings/church conferences after proper offering and notification described above.

docs.title: Active and Participating Member of First Baptist Church of Corsicana
docs.text:
A church member that is actively participating in the life of this local church. Several examples of activity in church life are:

  • Regular attendance at Sunday Services,
  • Regular attendance in Wednesday night activities, Bible studies, etc.,
  • Singing in the choir,
  • Attending or teaching in Sunday School,
  • Tithing,
  • Helping with Vacation Bible School,
  • Helping with security,
  • Working with Good News Clubs or Good News Café,
  • Visitation program,
  • Helping with children's or youth programs on Wednesday nights

This is not meant to be an all-inclusive list, but is a sample of the kind of activities available to active, participating members.

docs.title: Budget Committee
docs.text:
A joint committee comprised of the following:

Finance Committee, Senior Pastor, Men's Ministry Coordinator, Women's Ministry Coordinator, Program Coordinator, Financial Secretary, Ministerial Staff, Church Treasurer, Chairpersons of Standing Committees.

Others may be included as needed when they have requests for budgeted monies.

  1. Presented to Deacon Council on May 11, 2023. ↩

  2. Amendment Approved. First Vote June 14, 2020. Second Vote July 12, 2020. Version 1.4 ↩

  3. Amendment Approved. First Vote November 11, 2011. Second Vote December 7, 2011. Version 1.3 ↩

docs.title: Constitution
docs.text:
The Constitution establishes the foundational principles and governing framework of the church.

It defines the church’s purpose, doctrinal commitments, membership, and organizational structure. All other governing documents, including bylaws and policies, are subordinate to and must be consistent with the Constitution.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
The Constitution addresses matters such as:

  • The mission and purpose of the church
  • Doctrinal beliefs and theological commitments
  • Church membership
  • Church governance and leadership structure
  • The relationship between the church and its officers

docs.title: Authority
docs.text: The Constitution is the highest governing document of the church. Any amendments must be adopted in accordance with the procedures defined within the document itself.

docs.title: Related Documents
docs.text:
• Church Bylaws
  • Approved Policies

docs.title: Constitution of First Baptist Church of Corsicana, Texas
Preamble
docs.text: With this constitution we, the members of First Baptist Church of Corsicana seek to ensure the preservation and security of the principles of our faith, the governing of this body in an orderly manner, consistent with the accepted tenets of Texas Baptists, and the Baptist General Convention of Texas. We seek to preserve the liberties inherent in each individual member of this church and the freedom of this body with respect to its relationship to other churches of the same faith, and the performance of its task of reaching the world with the Gospel of Jesus Christ, we do declare and establish this constitution.

docs.title: Article One. Name
docs.text: The name of this organization shall be First Baptist Church of Corsicana, Texas.

docs.title: Article Two. Purpose
docs.text: The purpose of this organization shall be to worship the Eternal God, to follow the leadership and teaching of Jesus Christ as revealed in the Scriptures, to live out His purpose under the guidance and power of the Holy Spirit, and to fulfill the mission of sharing the gospel of our Lord with all mankind.

docs.title: Article Three. Membership, Faith, and Practice
Membership
docs.text: The membership of this church shall consist of persons who have accepted Jesus Christ as Savior and Lord, who have followed Him in scriptural baptism by immersion administered either by this church or some other church of like faith and order, and whose names appear on the membership roll of this church.

docs.title: Faith and Practice
docs.text: The standard of faith and practice shall be that set forth in the New Testament and incorporated in the Articles of Faith as outlined in the Baptist Faith and Message, a statement adopted in 1963.

docs.title: Article Four. Government
docs.text:
It is agreed that the intention of this body is to discover and follow the will of Christ, its Head, in the making of decisions and taking of action. To this end, the active Government of the church shall be vested in the body of believers who compose its membership, and it shall be subject to the control of no other body. The church shall, to achieve this purpose, adopt appropriate by-laws in definitive form to implement the Baptist doctrine of local autonomous church government.

This church accepts the Scriptures as its authority in all matter of faith and practice, and its understanding of Christian truth as contained therein, is in essential accord with the beliefs of the Baptist churches that cooperate with the Baptist General Convention of Texas, and Baptist churches that have historically cooperated with the Southern Baptist Convention.

docs.title: Article Five. Amendments
docs.text:
This constitution may be amended by the following procedures:

  1. Any proposed amendment shall be offered in writing at the regular business meeting of the church.
  2. The proposed amendment, together with the designated time for its consideration, shall be published twice in print or digital form, offered by the church thirty days prior to the meeting in which a vote is to be taken.
  3. The proposed amendment shall be approved by three-fourths (3/4) of members present and voting at each of two consecutive regular business meetings/church conferences after proper offering and notification described above.

  1. Presented to Deacon Council on May 11, 2023. ↩

docs.title: Procedures & How-To Guides
docs.text:
This section contains step-by-step instructions for common administrative and ministry tasks performed by support staff.

These guides are written for staff members who may not use certain tools regularly. Each guide walks through a specific task from start to finish.

docs.title: Available Guides
docs.text: • Name Tags — Mail Merge in LibreOffice Writer Create and print personalized name tags for VBS, camp, or other events using a spreadsheet and LibreOffice Writer.

docs.title: Suggestions
docs.text: If you need a procedure that isn't documented here, contact the Minister of Administration.

docs.title: Name Tags — Mail Merge in LibreOffice Writer
docs.text:
The goal of this procedure is to produce a document containing multiple name tags — one per person — ready to send to the printer and then cut apart. Names are printed directly on the tags; no handwriting required.

This guide uses LibreOffice Writer on a standard US Letter page (8.5" × 11"). The same process can be adapted to other page sizes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure the following are in place:

  • LibreOffice Writer — for designing and producing the name tag document
  • LibreOffice Calc — for editing your data file
  • LibreOffice Base — must be installed; it works behind the scenes to connect your data to the Writer document. The merge will not work without it.
  • Your data file — a CSV or ODS spreadsheet with participant names and any other fields you want on the tag. This file must be complete and closed before you begin.

docs.title: Step 1 — Prepare Your Data File
docs.text: Your data can come from any source: a form export (ShelbyNext, Google Forms, etc.), a manually built list, or a combination. The final file needs to be a CSV or ODS spreadsheet with column headers in Row 1.

docs.title: Recommended field names
docs.text:
Use short, single-word headers with no spaces:

Field Use FirstName First name LastName Last name Group Class, team, grade, or other grouping Age Age Grade Grade level Phone Phone number Gender Gender

Include only the fields you actually need on the tag. A simple name tag might only need FirstName and LastName.

docs.title: Before you move on
docs.text:
• Edit and clean your data file in LibreOffice Calc.
  • Finalize the file completely before registering it. If you edit the file after registering it as a data source, LibreOffice will not automatically pick up your changes — you will need to re-register it (see gotcha below).
  • Save the file and close it before opening Writer.
  • Note the file location — you will need to browse for it.

Duplicate data source entries

If you re-register the same file more than once (for example, after editing it), LibreOffice will create a new data source entry each time and append a number to the name — vbs_data, then vbs_data1, then vbs_data2, and so on. This is normal; just make sure you select the correct (most recent) entry when prompted.

docs.title: Step 2 — Open Your Writer Document
docs.text: Open LibreOffice Writer. You can start with a blank document or an existing one. You will design the name tag layout later — for now, just have the document open.

docs.title: Step 3 — Launch the Mail Merge Wizard and Register Your Data Source
docs.text: From the menu bar, choose Tools → Mail Merge Wizard. The Mail Merge Wizard dialog will open. The left side of the dialog lists the steps: Select Starting Document, Select Document Type, Insert Address Block, Create Salutation, and Adjust Layout.

docs.title: Step 1 of the wizard — Select Starting Document
docs.text:
On the right side, select Use the current document.

Before clicking Next, you must register your data file. Below the Use the current document radio button, click the Exchange Databases… button. This opens the Exchange Databases dialog.

docs.title: Registering your data file
docs.text:
The Exchange Databases dialog has two panes: Databases in Use (left) and Available Databases (right).

  1. Click the Browse button below the Available Databases pane.
  2. A file finder dialog will open. Navigate to your CSV or ODS file, select it, and click Open.
  3. The file will appear in the Available Databases pane as a LibreOffice Base file (LibreOffice creates this Base file as an intermediary — it is what actually connects to your Writer document).
  4. Click the expansion arrow next to the file name to expand it. You will see the data table inside.
  5. Click on the table to select it. The Define button at the bottom of the dialog will become active once a valid table is selected.
  6. Click Define. The dialog closes and you are returned to the Mail Merge Wizard.

Editing your data file after registering

LibreOffice Base acts as an intermediary between your original data file and Writer. If you edit your original CSV or ODS after registering, the Base file will not automatically reflect those changes. You must re-register the file to pick up any edits.

docs.title: Step 2 of the wizard — Select Document Type
docs.text: Select Letter and click Next.

docs.title: Step 3 of the wizard — Insert Address Block
docs.text: No action needed here (this step is designed for formal letters). Confirm that the data source name shown looks correct, then click Next.

docs.title: Step 4 of the wizard — Create Salutation
docs.text:
No action needed. Do not check the salutation checkbox.

Click Finish, not Next

The Next button on this step is greyed out unless you check the salutation box. Since you do not need a salutation, click Finish instead of Next. Steps 5 and beyond in the wizard do not apply to this task.

Clicking Finish closes the wizard and returns you to your document. The Mail Merge toolbar will appear.

docs.title: Step 4 — Design Your Name Tag Layout
Adjust page margins
docs.text: From the menu bar, choose Format → Page Style (or press Alt+Shift+P). Set your margins to zero or near-zero on all sides. You will control name tag padding through the table settings in the next step, so minimal page margins give you the most usable space.

docs.title: Insert a table
docs.text:
From the menu bar, choose Table → Insert Table. Either of these layouts gives you 6 name tags per sheet:

  • Portrait — 2 columns × 3 rows
  • Landscape — 3 columns × 2 rows

docs.title: Set table properties
docs.text:
From the menu bar, choose Table → Properties. Work through the tabs as follows:

Text Flow tab

  • Uncheck Allow table to split across pages and columns. This keeps each name tag intact on its page.

Borders tab

  • Set Padding to at least 0.25" on all sides. This creates the inner margin for name tag content.
  • Leave Line Arrangement set to No Borders (default). You will cut apart the tags, so printed borders are generally not needed. Add borders here if your design calls for them.

Background tab

  • No changes needed unless you want a background color or image on the tags.

Click OK to close Table Properties.

docs.title: Set row height
docs.text:
1. Select the entire table: Table → Select → Table.
  2. Set the row height: Table → Size → Row Height.
  3. Enter a value in the Height field.

For a 3-row table on an 11" page, the mathematical maximum is about 3.67" per row — but entering that value will push the table off the page and cause problems (see gotcha below). Use 3.5" or less.

Row height and blank pages

If your row height pushes the table even slightly past the bottom of the page, LibreOffice will add a blank overflow page. In the final merged document, that blank page will appear between every page of name tags — a significant problem. Stay at 3.5" or less for a 3-row portrait layout.

docs.title: Mock up your label
docs.text:
Before inserting merge fields, type sample text inside the first cell to mock up the layout. Use real-looking names and text so you can judge how the content actually fits.

Why mock up first

Merge field placeholders like <FirstName> are long and will appear to overflow the cell — they do not reflect actual name lengths. A mock-up with real text lets you dial in font sizes before the fields go in.

Use LibreOffice's built-in heading styles to create a natural size hierarchy:

  • Heading 1 — primary line (e.g., first name or full name)
  • Heading 2 — secondary line (e.g., group or class)
  • Heading 3 — additional lines as needed

Using styles means you can adjust font sizes globally with a single style edit rather than reformatting each cell individually.

Apply styles from bottom to top

As you apply heading styles, the larger font sizes will push lines out of the visible cell area. If you apply styles top to bottom, you will lose sight of the lines you haven't styled yet. Work from the last line upward so each styled line pushes content you have already handled, and everything remains visible as you go.

Work with the mock-up until the layout looks right. If the sizing is off after you run a test merge, you can adjust individual styles at that point.

docs.title: Add a background image (optional)
docs.text:
If you want an image behind the text — a logo, watermark, or decorative graphic — insert it inside the cell before adding merge fields. A transparent PNG works best.

  1. Click inside the cell where you want the image.
  2. From the menu bar, choose Insert → Image and select your PNG file.
  3. With the image selected, open Image Properties: Format → Image → Properties (or press F4).
  4. On the Wrap tab:
  5. Under Settings, select Through. This allows text to print across the image.
  6. Under Options, check In background. This places the image behind the text layer.
  7. Under Options, Allow overlap is typically checked by default — leave it as is.
  8. Resize and position the image within the cell as needed.

Then replace the sample text with merge fields, or insert the fields alongside the sample text and apply the same styles to them.

docs.title: Step 5 — Insert Merge Fields
docs.text:
Click inside the first cell of the table. From the menu bar, choose Insert → Field → More Fields (or press Ctrl+F2).

The Fields dialog will open with six tabs. Choose the Database tab.

  • On the left pane (Type), select Mail Merge Fields.
  • On the right pane (Database Selection), your table should already be selected from Step 3.
  • Under the table name, you will see your field names.
  • Select a field name and click Insert (or double-click the field name). The field will appear in the cell as <FieldName>.
  • Insert all the fields you want, pressing Enter between lines and Space between fields on the same line.
  • Apply heading styles to the fields to match your mock-up.
  • Close the Fields dialog when done.

docs.title: Next Record field
docs.text:
For the merge to advance to the next person in each subsequent cell, you must insert a Next Record field at the beginning of every cell after the first.

To insert it: Insert → Field → More Fields → Database tab → select Next record from the Type list → click Insert.

Next Record in the first cell

You may also insert the Next Record field in the first cell without skipping the first record, but verify your output carefully to confirm all names are present.

docs.title: Copy to remaining cells
docs.text:
Once the first cell is formatted and complete:

  1. Select the entire first cell.
  2. Copy it (Ctrl+C).
  3. Click into the next cell and paste (Ctrl+V).
  4. Repeat for all remaining cells.
  5. If you did not include the Next Record field in the first cell, add it to the beginning of each subsequent cell now.

docs.title: Step 6 — Save the Merged Document
docs.text:
When your layout is complete, use the Mail Merge toolbar to produce the final document.

Mail Merge toolbar

The toolbar includes navigation buttons (First, Previous, Current, Next, Last entry), an Exclude Recipient checkbox, and output buttons. The Current Mail Merge Entry field should show -1 before running the merge. If it shows 1, the first record may be skipped.

Click Save Merged Documents on the toolbar. The Save As Options dialog will open:

  1. Select Save as a single large document (default).
  2. Optionally, specify a record range if you only need to print a subset of the name tags.
  3. Click Save Documents.

A small progress dialog will appear as LibreOffice works through the records. When it finishes, a file dialog will open — choose a location and file name and click Save.

docs.title: Step 7 — Review and Print
docs.text:
Open the saved merged document and review it before printing:

  • Confirm that names are filling in correctly and nothing is truncated.
  • Check that the number of pages looks right for your record count.
  • Look for any unexpected blank pages (a sign of a row height problem — see Step 4 gotcha).

When everything looks correct, load your paper into the printer and choose File → Print.

docs.title: Reusing the Template
docs.text:
Save your Writer document (the one with merge fields, not the merged output) as a template for future events. To reuse it:

  1. Open the saved Writer template.
  2. Launch the Mail Merge Wizard (Tools → Mail Merge Wizard).
  3. On Step 1, click Exchange Databases… and register the new event's data file.
  4. Proceed through the wizard and save the merged document as before.

You do not need to rebuild the table layout from scratch each time.
